introduction: Enlarged or varicose veins of the tissues at the anus or rectum. Engorgement of the vascular complex or thrombus often leads to the symptoms of disease. May be external or internal.

features: May present with bright red rectal bleeding
Often become thrombosed and protrude into the anal canal.
When thrombosed, causes acute perineal pain with or without necrosis and bleeding on defeacation
Fibrosed external haemorrhoids present as anal tags

drug: Suppositories/ointments of preparations containing hydrocortisone acetate with or without lidocaine hydrochloride plus astringent(s)

definitive_treatment: Injection Sclerotherapy, photocoagulaton, cauterization, cryosurgery are other modalities of treatment.
